layout.pattern: Headline KPI strip on top; supporting charts ordered by importance.
layout.primary_section: Headline metrics for Onboarding Funnel and Average Order Value
layout.secondary_section: Trend and comparison charts for the remaining KPIs

styling.number_format: percentages to 1 decimal; large values with K/M suffix
styling.accessibility: maintain >= 4.5:1 contrast; never rely on color alone

rationales.claim: Use a sankey chart for Onboarding Funnel.
rationales.principle: Sankey diagrams encode volume of flow between stages by link width.

rationales.claim: Use a stacked_bar chart for Average Order Value.
rationales.principle: Stacked bars show how a total divides into parts within each category.

rationales.claim: Use a donut chart for Revenue by Category.
rationales.principle: Angular/area encodings communicate proportions for a small number of categories.

rationales.claim: Use a line chart for Revenue.
rationales.principle: Position along a common time axis is the most accurate encoding for temporal trends.

rationales.claim: Limit each view to 5-7 elements and group related KPIs.
rationales.principle: Managing cognitive load keeps the dashboard scannable (Few 2006).

layout.secondary_section: Trend and comparison charts for the remaining KPIs

styling.number_format: percentages to 1 decimal; large values with K/M suffix
styling.accessibility: maintain >= 4.5:1 contrast; never rely on color alone

rationales.claim: Use a bar chart for Engagement Rate.
rationales.principle: Bar length supports precise magnitude comparison across discrete categories.

rationales.claim: Use a line chart for Lead Volume.
rationales.principle: Position along a common time axis is the most accurate encoding for temporal trends.

rationales.claim: Use a sankey chart for Lead Funnel.
rationales.principle: Sankey diagrams encode volume of flow between stages by link width.

rationales.claim: Limit each view to 5-7 elements and group related KPIs.
rationales.principle: Managing cognitive load keeps the dashboard scannable (Few 2006).
